title: papaver coloradense var. typicum
intro: Papaver coloradense var. typicum, also known as Colorado poppy, is a variant of the Papaver coloradense plant, a species of flowering plant in the poppy family. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, particularly meconic acid. The health benefits of Papaver coloradense var. typicum include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antimicrobial activity. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
